questions: You say what you mean and answer questions directly. Your manager asks if you've heard back from the client you were supposed to follow up with on Tuesday.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. A family member asks if you remembered to mail the form they gave you two weeks ago.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. Your partner asks if you made the appointment you said you'd make last week.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. A coworker asks if you finished running the numbers for tomorrow's meeting.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. Your manager asks if you understood the new process they just walked you through.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. A friend asks if you've read the article they sent you last week.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. A coworker asks if you've met the new person who just joined the team.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. A coworker asks if you've used the new scheduling tool they rolled out last week.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. A colleague asks if you submitted the expense report before the deadline.
You say what you mean and answer questions directly. Your friend asks if you called the mechanic about your car like you said you would.
